repo_prior_art.cite: doc/API.md §3.1 has only Manski-family entries; failed pid_late_bounded_defiers_one_sided_compliance omitted the full four-cell cap polytope

published_prior_art.cite: BalkePearl1997 and SachsJonzonSjolanderGabriel2023/DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2024 cover finite LP/symbolic sharp-bounds existence, not the exact two-cap four-cell object

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_late_bounded_defiers_one_sided_compliance was rejected for missing the Z=1,D=0 cell; pid_dynamic_iv_compliance_v2 is dynamic, not this static two-cap ATE envelope

published_prior_art.cite: RichardsonRobins2010 gives binary-IV geometry/fixed-defier comparison, but I found no stated necessary-and-sufficient never-taker-cap strict-shrinkage frontier
BalkePearl1997 and RichardsonRobins2010 use latent response distributions for sharpness; this is new only insofar as it certifies the proposed two-cap facet catalogue

repo_prior_art.cite: imbens_angrist_late: Wald/LATE coordinate; manski_nonparametric_bounds: bounded-outcome support face

published_prior_art.cite: ImbensAngrist1994 identifies complier LATE; Manski1990 gives the bounded-outcome support-fill-in interval

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The displayed equivalence optimizes B_L(t) and B_U(t) over all t in I_pi, but B_L/B_U were defined only for outcome-feasible fixed-defier shares; restrict to the outcome-feasible t-domain or define infeasible values explicitly.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Define an outcome-feasible interval or set I_pi^Y(P,delta_bar,nu_bar) and use it consistently in the Richardson-Robins fixed-t equivalence display.
Add a named boundary corollary for the frontier: give one explicit observable inequality pattern where the never-taker cap is redundant and one where it strictly moves an endpoint.
In Stage 0, separate the facet catalogue into base, single-cap, joint-cap, and support-fill-in tables with active-normal labels so the Levis-style nonsmooth inference consumer is concrete.

recommended_next_step: Patch the fixed-t feasibility domain in Conjecture 1, then proceed to Stage 0 facet enumeration.
